stop_reason: Campaign has achieved excellent performance (76.24% yield) with clear mechanistic understanding, but BO model fitting has failed for 12 consecutive iterations due to one-hot embedding dimensionality. Reconfiguration was attempted at iteration 18 but failed validation due to persistent ModelFittingErrors. With 47.5% budget remaining (21/40 iterations), continuing would waste experiments on random fallback proposals that consistently select suboptimal candidates (Cs+, pivalate, poor solvents). The optimal condition is well-characterized: Xantphos-type ligand, K+ acetate, butyl acetate, 120°C, 0.153M. Further improvements would require fixing the embedding method (switching to descriptor-based), but this structural change is beyond current configuration capabilities. Stopping now preserves resources while delivering a high-yielding, scientifically validated process.

final_config.rationale: Stable default BoTorch BO stack for Phase 1.

latest_review.reasoning: Matern52 remains theoretically appropriate for chemical space with sharp categorical transitions. The 12 consecutive fitting failures stem from one-hot encoding producing sparse high-dimensional vectors, not kernel inadequacy. However, reconfiguration cannot resolve this without changing the embedding method entirely, which requires system-level changes beyond kernel selection.

conclusion: The campaign completed after 19 experiments. Best result: 76.2400 with candidate {"base_SMILES": "O=C([O-])C.[K+]", "concentration": "0.153", "ligand_SMILES": "CC(C1=C(C2=CC=CC=C2P(C3CCCCC3)C4CCCCC4)C(C(C)C)=CC(C(C)C)=C1)C", "solvent_SMILES": "CCCCOC(C)=O", "temperature": "120"}.
